title: quercus urbani f. parvifolia
intro: Quercus urbani f. parvifolia, also known as the Philippine evergreen oak or Philippine white oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to the Philippines. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus urbani f. parvifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
